task_completion.description: Verify that a Synthetic EEG session is running in the OpenBCI GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI is open and the system status or top control bar indicates that the data source is 'Synthetic' or simulated.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard is configured to a 6-panel layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main workspace of the OpenBCI GUI is divided into exactly 6 distinct widget panels.

task_completion.description: Verify all 6 required widgets are present in the panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The titles/headers of the 6 visible panels must include: 'Time Series', 'FFT Plot', 'Band Power', 'Accelerometer', 'Focus', and 'Head Plot'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Notch filter is configured to 60 Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The hardware settings panel or top control bar shows the 'Notch' filter setting explicitly set to '60 Hz'.

task_completion.description: Verify Expert Mode is enabled in the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Expert Mode' button or toggle in the GUI interface (often near the top right or settings menu) is highlighted, checked, or shown as active.

task_completion.description: Verify evidence that the configuration was saved and a screenshot was taken.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for on-screen GUI notifications/logs indicating settings were saved and a screenshot was captured (e.g., via the 'm' key), or a file manager/terminal window showing the saved configuration file in '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Settings/' and the screenshot file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI rather than just editing configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the graphical interface of OpenBCI GUI being manipulated, rather than just a text editor modifying a JSON/XML settings file.

integrity.description: Verify the dashboard is actively displaying data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Time Series and FFT plots should display active waveforms and data traces, not completely empty or flatlined graphs, proving the synthetic session was actually started.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the UI configuration using terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands that directly echo or copy pre-made configuration files into the OpenBCI settings directory to skip manual GUI setup.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 running a SYNTHETIC data session. The dashboard must have exactly 6 panels containing: Time Series, FFT Plot, Band Power, Accelerometer, Focus, and Head Plot. The Notch filter must be set to 60 Hz and Expert Mode must be enabled. The agent must save the settings to ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Settings/ and capture a screenshot (typically using the 'm' key in OpenBCI).
